title: terebinthus rhoifolia
intro: Terebinthus rhoifolia, also known as the Rhus tree or Sumac, is a deciduous tree native to Asia and Europe, characterized by its compound leaves and small white flowers. The medicinal properties of Terebinthus rhoifolia come from its resin, which contains tannins, flavonoids, and volatile oils, providing anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of this plant include reducing inflammation, relieving pain, and treating skin conditions such as eczema and acne, as well as supporting cardiovascular health and immune function. Terebinthus rhoifolia can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in traditional medicine to treat various conditions, including fever, cough, and digestive issues.
